changes.result: HARDENING NOTE — CURSOR MUST IMPLEMENT FULL TARGET ORDER
changes.citation: Prompt §Target default page hierarchy requires: 1-Hero, 2-PrimaryFinding, 3-WorkingWell, 4-HealthSystems. Current page.tsx has WorkingWell (BalancedSystemsSummary) at line 708 BEFORE both Wave1DomainCards (717) and PrimaryFindingAndWhy (722). Fully implementing the target hierarchy requires moving PrimaryFindingAndWhy BEFORE BalancedSystemsSummary, not merely before Wave1DomainCards. Cursor must implement the complete target sequence and update feR2ResultsJourneyOrder.ts consistently. A partial fix moving only Wave1DomainCards after PrimaryFinding while leaving WorkingWell ahead of PrimaryFinding does NOT satisfy the target hierarchy in the prompt.

frontend/app/lib/feR2ResultsJourneyOrder.ts:5-14 — 8 canonical section test IDs (indices 0-7). Wave1DomainCards currently has NO entry. After the reorder Cursor must either: (a) add a test ID entry for Wave1DomainCards in the registry and assign it via data-testid in page.tsx, OR (b) explicitly confirm Wave1DomainCards intentionally has no journey-order tracking ID and document why. The section order test (required test #1: 'Primary finding before Health Systems Cards') must reference either these test IDs or the component render order — Cursor must ensure the test is reliable and not dependent on text matching or DOM structure that could change.

frontend/tests/components/StaleResultBanner.test.tsx — exists with 2 tests covering 'current' (renders null) and 'stale' (shows banner). Missing test for 'incompatible' (sprint test requirement #3). No FE_R2_RESULTS_JOURNEY_SECTION_TEST_IDS referenced in any test file — no existing section order test (sprint test requirement #1: 'Results page renders Primary finding before Health Systems Cards'). automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:248-278 — allowed Layer B changes enumerated (section ordering metadata, consumer-safe labels, result versioning display state, presentation metadata, deduplicated lead summary). Allowed Layer C changes enumerated (reorder rendered sections, banner, hide marker numeric scores, approved static UI labels). STOP condition #3 (line 420) protects against scope creep into broad backend narrative compiler redesign. Scope is clearly bounded.

Sprint is explicitly prohibited from touching: SignalEvaluator, SignalRegistry, root-cause compiler logic, clinical scoring thresholds, rail score calculations, PSI status (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:341-359). Allowed Layer B changes are DTO/presentation metadata additions only — not analytical logic changes. STOP condition #2 (line 419): STOP if completing the sprint requires frontend clinical inference. STOP condition #3 (line 420): STOP if completing the sprint requires broad backend narrative compiler redesign. These protections are comprehensive.

Section reorder is Layer C JSX order — no determinism impact. Banner guard change is a boolean condition change — no determinism impact. Label changes ('Strong Signal'→'Needs attention' etc.) are static UI string substitutions — no determinism impact. Marker score visibility is a display toggle — no determinism impact. Any Layer B DTO additions add new fields without modifying existing score calculations. Determinism is not at risk for the defined scope.

All label changes in prompt §Scope item 5 are static string substitutions: 'Trust strip'→'Data quality', 'Why this lead won'→'How confident is this read?', 'Strong Signal'→'Needs attention'. These are display-layer renames, not clinical priority decisions. Backend-decided fields must drive any ordering. STOP condition #2 (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:419) fires if frontend must derive clinical priority from raw marker data, signal IDs, score values, or string matching. Cursor must confirm at sprint close that no clinical inference was added.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:341-342 — explicitly prohibited: 'do not add a regenerate button in this sprint, do not implement regeneration flow'. Sprint test requirement #4: 'No regenerate button is introduced.' StaleResultBanner.tsx:30-32 existing conditional for regeneration_available must be preserved as-is. Cursor must not introduce any regeneration UI or backend hooks.

changes.result: NO STOP CONDITIONS TRIGGERED IN HARDENING
changes.citation: Pre-hardening assessment: (1) required audit files all exist — no STOP; (2) banner fix and section reorder do not require frontend clinical inference — no STOP; (3) banner fix is frontend-only, section reorder is layout-only — no broad narrative compiler redesign required — no STOP; (4) reorder/hide/rename does not remove clinically meaningful analysis — no STOP; (5) hiding marker numeric scores does not change clinical scoring semantics — no STOP; (6) banner fix does not require implementing regeneration flow — no STOP; (7) ARCH-RT-6 validator passed at LAUNCH-CORE-4 audit time, scope does not touch guardrail targets — no STOP; (8) test requirements are achievable (banner tests via unit test, section order via component or integration test) — no STOP. All STOP conditions clear.

Primary scope creep risk: Cursor may be tempted to implement backend DTO changes that require modifying report_compiler_v1.py or domain_score_assembler.py analytical logic rather than adding new presentation-only fields. STOP condition #3 (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:420) prohibits broad narrative compiler redesign. Cursor must limit Layer B changes to additive presentation metadata — if a DTO change requires modifying existing score or narrative assembly logic, STOP and report.

Out-of-scope list (automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:341-359) prohibits: regenerate button, regeneration backend flow, clinical scoring thresholds, rail score calculations, biomarker SSOT, unit conversion, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, PSI status, root-cause compiler logic, compiled artefact clinical content, package clinical content, frontend clinical inference, raw internal IDs/source traces, fallback parsers. Closure requirements (lines 483-490) enforce: no clinical interpretation logic changed, no meaningful analysis removed, no helper scripts committed.
